channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'All modern operating systems do this, including macOS and Linux': Microsoft exec fires back at critics accusing it of 'cheating' with Windows 11 speed boost feature
item.description: Microsoft defends CPU trick to boost Windows 11 performance after critics label it a fudged fix.

item.title: Finally, texts between Android and iPhone users can be end-to-end encrypted
item.description: Google had urged Apple for years to support RCS texting to make communication between each company's devices more seamless.

item.title: New water-powered tech could power batteries that can last hundreds of years without degrading — and are so safe that the electrolytes can be used as 'tofu-brine' for home cooking
item.description: Chinese scientists developed a non-toxic water battery capable of surviving 120,000 cycles using corrosion-resistant organic polymer structures.

item.title: iOS 26.5 is here: How to get it, top new features
item.description: Apple rolled out iOS 26.5 on May 11. Here's what's new and how to upgrade your iPhone.

item.title: TikTok is going ad-free in the UK — if you pay £3.99 a month for it
item.description: TikTok is launched an ad-free service is in the UK, but would you pay £3.99 for it?

item.title: A new Erewhon competitor just opened in West Hollywood with no marketing or social media. It’s counting on you to post about it
item.description: High-end ‘hypebeast’ grocers are selling a lifestyle with help from the power of TikTok. Laurel Supply is the newest kid on the block. A $20 smoothie and a $19 single strawberry could only belong in one place: Erewhon, the luxury grocery chain and celebrity hot spot in Los Angeles.

item.title: Will micro-LED ever really replace OLED? The next-gen TV tech takes a blow as Samsung's TV boss pulls back from investing in making it — it's a bad sign, but will TCL and Hisense keep the flag flying?
item.description: Micro-LED TV looks amazing, but it's seriously expensive –so much so that Samsung appears to be scaling back its plans

item.title: Prime Video is copying Netflix with its own vertical video feed for movies and shows — but the TikTokification of streaming platforms isn’t what movie buffs are asking for
item.description: Prime Video is rolling out its new Clips vertical video feed for mobile, following Netflix and Disney+.

item.title: These head-spinning Wordle statistics show why the New York Times is turning the game into an NBC TV show
item.description: It’s evidence of just how much of a cultural phenomenon the newspaper’s ‘Games’ category has become. Your family group chat’s favorite daily word game is about to get an adaptation for the screen.

item.title: Intelligence artificielle | « Le Canada n’a pas un problème d’offre, mais un problème de demande »
item.description: Le Canada n’a pas un problème d’offre en intelligence artificielle et innovation, mais un problème de demande et devrait notamment s’inspirer des Américains pour le régler, selon un réputé entrepreneur techno québécois.

item.title: 9 college-ready laptops and tablets that make the perfect high school graduation gift
item.description: Top tech to set them up for college success — all tried and tested by TechRadar experts.

item.title: iOS 26.5 : voici les 3 grandes nouveautés et quelques détails passés sous silence
item.description: Apple a déployé iOS 26.5 ce 11 mai 2026. Trois nouveautés sont présente, mais c'est surtout le chiffrement de bout en bout du RCS entre iPhone et Android qui marque cette mise à jour.

item.title: 'Addressing one of the most dangerous missions in ground combat': US Army robots (and AI) set to play a critical role in getting wounded soldiers off the battlefield without putting humans at risk
item.description: US Army troops tested robotic casualty evacuation vehicles alongside AI systems during military exercises designed for drone-saturated battlefields.
